definitions.text: Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
examples: The room was too dark for reading.
Dark signals should be treated as all-way stop signs.
Dark signals should be treated as all-way stop signs.

definitions.text: A complete or (more often) partial absence of light.

examples: We kept him in the dark.
The lawyer was left in the dark as to why the jury was dismissed.
It was after dark before we got to playing baseball.

definitions.text: To grow or become dark, darken.
To remain in the dark, lurk, lie hidden or concealed.
To make dark, darken; to obscure.
